objects.name: Analytic 1698
objects.description: A managed or supervised app, app update, or enterprise-distributed build retains a legitimate-seeming identity but exhibits post-delivery behavior inconsistent with its expected role, prior version, or distribution context. Because iOS exposes less direct visibility into bundled dependency tampering or component-level supply-chain insertion, the defender prioritizes supervised app inventory, signing/provisioning trust posture, entitlement and behavior drift after update, new sensor/resource use, and new downstream network effects soon after install or version change.

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: iOS:MDMLog
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Managed app distribution, supervised install posture, or provisioning trust context remains expected while a known app exhibits materially different behavior after version change

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: iOS:MDMLog
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Known application version declares, activates, or exhibits new entitlements, privacy permissions, or capability use inconsistent with prior baseline or business role

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobileEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Updated or newly delivered application wakes, foregrounds, refreshes, or becomes active shortly after version change with weak recent user interaction

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobileEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Known application begins first-seen or expanded use of protected frameworks, account services, background task APIs, crypto/network service APIs, or other runtime behaviors after update/install

x_mitre_mutable_elements.description: Maximum span between app install/version change and first suspicious post-delivery behavior.
Whether the analytic should only apply to supervised devices with high-confidence app inventory and managed distribution telemetry.
Approved apps expected to expand capabilities or contact new destinations because of legitimate releases.
Grace period after approved releases during which some behavior drift may be expected.
Whether certain behaviors should only be treated as suspicious when they occur without expected visible user interaction.
Threshold for distinguishing autonomous post-update behavior from expected user-driven first-run flows.
Expected new domains, APIs, telemetry services, or CDNs associated with approved app updates.
Threshold for how much entitlement or capability drift is tolerated for a known app.
